claim_boundary: Published cross-code reference values only (Dimits 2000). NOT re-run here and NOT a measured SCPN-CONTROL result. The saturated nonlinear CBC chi_i public claim remains BLOCKED: a long nonlinear campaign run (>= saturation) in the module's own normalisation, reconciled with these units, is required before any quantitative agreement claim.

cross_model_factors_at_base_case.note: Global code 2.4x lower than flux-tube (finite rho/a=1/160); IFS-PPPL 2.7x and 1994 gyrofluid 3.3x higher than flux-tube gyrokinetic at R/L_T=6.9.

dimits_shift.note: Dimits shift: nonlinear effective critical R/L_T (~6.0) exceeds the linear critical (~4.0) due to zonal-flow regulation.

name: Cyclone Base Case nonlinear ion heat diffusivity (published cross-code reference)

offset_linear_fit.equation: chi_i * L_n / (rho_i^2 * v_ti) = A * (1 - B * L_T/R)
offset_linear_fit.reference_eq: Dimits 2000 Eq. (1) (LLNL gyrokinetic flux-tube fit, ref [52])

reference: Dimits et al., Phys. Plasmas 7 (2000) 969 — Comparisons and physics basis of tokamak transport models and turbulence simulations
source_note: Author (Hammett) copy of the published paper; numeric values transcribed verbatim from the article text (Sec. 5, Eq. 1) via pdftotext, not read off figures.

units_contract.caveat: This is the paper's L_n-normalised gyro-Bohm-like unit. The module gk_nonlinear.chi_i_gB divides Q_i by R/L_Ti in a DIFFERENT gyro-Bohm normalisation; a direct numeric comparison to the module requires an explicit unit conversion (R/L_n factor and gyro-Bohm base) that is NOT applied here.
units_contract.chi_i_normalisation: chi_i * L_n / (rho_i^2 * v_ti)  [dimensionless, the units of the paper's Fig. 3]
